title: Anti-Gravity 2026 - Operational Workflows
audience: LinkedIn builders, engineering leaders, and AI operators

slides.headline: Anti-Gravity in 2026: __Beyond Basic Usage__
slides.subtitle: Practical workflows teams can run now
slides.accentText: Agent mode | MCP context | extension stack

slides.headline: What changed?
slides.accentText: From assistant chat to execution systems
bullets: Agent mode can plan and execute in one loop
MCP brings live tools and data into context
Extension ecosystems expand model choice

slides.headline: The stack that creates leverage
slides.introText: Real gains come from **agent mode + MCP + review discipline**
bullets: Use one control layer for planning and execution
Attach real systems instead of copy-paste context
Keep output auditable before shipping

cards.title: Execution Layer
cards.detail: Run scoped tasks with clear completion criteria.

cards.title: Data Layer
cards.detail: Connect live tools and sources through MCP.

cards.title: Control Layer
cards.detail: Review artifacts before merge or deploy.

slides.headline: Workflow 1: Data-aware engineering loop
slides.introText: Ground implementation decisions in **live schema context**
bullets: Inspect models and relationships before coding
Generate and run SQL in the same flow
Validate output against business semantics

cards.title: Schema Read
cards.detail: Start from trusted structures, not assumptions.

cards.title: Query Loop
cards.detail: Iterate faster with execution in context.

slides.headline: Workflow 2: Voice agent factory
slides.introText: Ship voice systems by splitting work into **specialist lanes**
bullets: Lane 1 defines telephony and webhook contracts
Lane 2 builds STT, orchestration, and tools
Lane 3 validates browser and API behavior

cards.title: Pipeline Build
cards.detail: Compose speech flow with modular tool calls.

slides.headline: Workflow 3: Multi-model extension cockpit
slides.introText: Keep AG as mission control and route tasks to the **best-fit model**
bullets: Use extension channels where specialization helps
Assign model by task type, not preference
Centralize review and release decisions

cards.title: Model Routing
cards.detail: Pick the right engine per task boundary.

cards.title: Extension Bridge
cards.detail: Use external tooling without fragmenting control.

cards.title: Release Gate
cards.detail: Approve through one operational command center.

slides.headline: Workflow 4: MCP reliability hardening
slides.introText: Treat connectors like infrastructure as your stack grows
bullets: Use gateway patterns to reduce reconnect churn
Standardize env and path configuration
Track failures and recovery loops per project

cards.title: Config Discipline
cards.detail: Use explicit variables and deterministic paths.

cards.title: Failure Ops
cards.detail: Observe and tune reconnect behavior continuously.

slides.headline: Adoption map for week one
slides.introText: Roll out in phases so teams gain confidence without chaos
bullets: Phase 1: single-agent tasks with review rituals
Phase 2: attach one MCP connector to delivery
Phase 3: split roles into specialist agents

chart.title: Operational Maturity by Phase
chart.footnote: Illustrative maturity index for sequencing adoption.

slides.headline: Ready to operationalize __Anti-Gravity__?
slides.ctaText: Comment AG and I will share the rollout map

caption:
Anti-Gravity is most useful when you treat it as an execution system, not just a coding chat surface.

This deck breaks down practical workflows teams can run today:
- Data-aware engineering loop
- Voice agent factory
- Multi-model extension cockpit
- MCP reliability hardening
- Week-one adoption map

If you want the exact implementation sequence I use with teams, comment AG.
